mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate keeps discount rates and financing costs suppressed, supporting equity valuations and buybacks. When FEDFUNDS stays near the lower bound, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive on average.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is below 1% for at least 3 consecutive months, or the effect turns negative in a rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in CPIAUCSL reduces real rates expectations and increases pressure for policy accommodation, which lifts risk assets over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation accelerates from a low base rather than from already high inflation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not produce positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if CPI spikes coincide with negative average returns after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that later feeds weaker consumption and earnings expectations. When unemployment increases quickly, equity prices should weaken over the following quarter as investors reprice cyclical cash flows.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of periods after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, or if the average return after the trigger is not below unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A broad pickup in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial output and stronger real activity, which should support earnings expectations and reduce recession risk. When industrial production is trending up, S&P 500 returns over the next month should be positive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO trend periods do not outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return rate over any 3-year rolling window, or if the sign of the average return is negative after repeated industrial expansion observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST reflects improving residential investment and household demand momentum, which feeds into construction, employment, and consumer spending. A sustained increase in housing starts should lead to positive SPX returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive after sustained HOUST increases, or if the hit rate falls below 50% in any 4-quarter rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth and RSAFS growth both decelerate together, labor income and retail spending weaken simultaneously, signaling lower near-term corporate revenue and margin pressure. This joint slowdown should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of joint PAYEMS and RSAFS weakening do not produce negative average 20-day returns, or if either series alone explains the effect with no incremental interaction contribution.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment shock combined with weak payroll creation identifies labor-market stress that is more informative than either series alone. If UNRATE is rising while PAYEMS is flat or falling, risk assets should decline over the next 5 trading days as recession odds are repriced.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months while PAYEMS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the interaction effect disappears after excluding payroll revisions.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS paired with slowing CPIAUCSL signals a policy mistake risk: real rates rise while inflation decelerates, compressing valuations and hurting equities. In that regime, S&P 500 returns over the next quarter should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if, during tightening cycles, the interaction does not predict negative 60-day returns better than chance, or if positive returns dominate when FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL decelerates.
